Also, when you're in the Write window, you can toggle the "Contacts" button to show a mini-address book - select an entry and double-click or click the "Add to" an entry to add it to the message.

content: Where are your contacts stored? Are they in Google Contacts? If so, open the Address Book, click on the little icon to the left of "New Contact" and choose "Add CardDAV Address Book". Enter your Gmail credentials if prompted. Otherwise, you may have to export them from wherever they are now stored, transfer them to the laptop, and import them into Thunderbird. If you have a choice of export formats, choose VCF (vCard) format - it's much simpler and less error-prone.

content:
If your account is IMAP, then this is exactly what's supposed to happen. Basically an IMAP email client like Thunderbird mirrors everything what's happening on the server. If you prefer to remove messages from the server POP may be the better choice for you. https://support.mozilla.org/kb/faq-changing-imap-pop

When the behavior you're seeing now isn't what you want, then I'm wondering why you set up your account as IMAP in the first place.
creator: christ1

content:
Thank you christ1!

I did not know the difference between IMAP and POP, when I set up Thunderbird.

If I choose POP, then messages will automatically sync, however, when I delete messages in Yahoo, the messages will not be deleted in Thunderbird?
creator: tranland

content: With POP there is no sync. POP can only download messages from Inbox. You can tell Thunderbird to either remove messages from the server, or leave them on the server, after downloading. That's all. Any directory structure you may have created on the server would stay there, but becomes inaccessible for the email client. You should really make yourself familiar with the differences between POP and IMAP protocols.
creator: christ1

content:
Try doing it over this way:

Copy TB files from the laptop

- launch TB on the laptop

- go to TB menu > Help > Troubleshooting Information

- click the Application Basics > Profile Folder 'Open Folder' button

- the profile folder will open

- navigate three levels up to the AppData\Roaming folder

- close TB

- copy the AppData\Roaming\Thunderbird folder to a flash drive

---

Paste the copied TB files to the PC

- open the AppData\Roaming folder like you did above

- delete the Thunderbird folder, or copy it somewhere if you want to save it

- replace it with the Thunderbird folder from the flash drive

- launch TB

content:
Si, se Thunderbird ha tutte le mail che vuoi ripristinare, puoi farlo. Ma come procedere dipende dalla tua configurazione, ed in parte dalle impostazioni di Gmail. Prova a fornire queste informazioni:

  1. Versione di Thunderbird
  2. Versione del sistema operativo
  3. Provider delle caselle email (Gmail, Libero, Hotmail, etc.)
  4. Tipologia di account (POP3, IMAP)
  5. Estensioni installate
  6. Messaggi di errore che vengono visualizzati durante l'utilizzo di Thunderbird
  7. Software di sicurezza eventualmente presenti (Antivirus e simili)
  8. Screenshot del problema (se utile a chiarire il contesto)

content: The simplest solution is to use a theme. Click tools>addons&themes and then click 'themes' in left column. Next, in the search box, enter a color and themes focused on that color will appear. You can install, evaluate, and remove easily. It is also possible by setting up custom CSS, but that's not trivial.
